mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures household consumption momentum; higher sales directly lift corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression of 20-day SPX returns on RSAFS changes yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% signals slack in the labor market that reduces household income growth and compresses corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate jointly combined with falling housing starts reduces credit availability and construction employment, transmitting negative shocks to broad corporate earnings and pushing S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST interaction term does not exhibit a negative coefficient (p < 0.05) in a 20-day forward return regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated policy rates, a declining unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that increases the probability of further monetary tightening, raising discount rates and dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, episodes of falling UNRATE are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not predict statistically significant positive 5-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained double-digit decline in housing starts over a three-month window signals collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After HOUST declines more than 10% over 3 months, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is nonnegative on average (t-test p > 0.05 for difference from zero).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation jointly combined with slowing payroll growth compresses real wages and squeezes corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and PAYEMS changes fails to produce a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.05) in a 20-day forward return regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp three-month increase in industrial production exceeding 2% signals a V-shaped manufacturing recovery that boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises more than 2% over 3 months, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in more than half of triggered episodes.
